title: GetSign Just Got Even Better: New Features to Power Your Document Workflows in monday.com
excerpt: We’ve been working hard to make GetSign more powerful and flexible for your document workflows inside monday.com. Here are the latest features we just released that will help teams streamline signatures, document creatio…

posts.author: randhirjetpack (Randhir Chaudhary)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve been working hard to make GetSign more powerful and flexible for your document workflows inside monday.com. Here are the latest features we just released that will help teams streamline signatures, document creation, and template access like never before.

Subitems Support for GetSign is Now Live

GetSign is now fully compatible with subitems on monday.com. You can send documents for signature or generate them using data directly from subitems.

This makes it easier to manage nested workflows, such as:

  • Onboarding checklists

  • Line-item quotes

  • Approval modules inside tasks

Set Expiry on Signing Links

You can now configure an expiration date when sending signing links. Once the link expires, signatories will no longer be able to access the document.

You can also reset the process from your end and send a fresh request with or without a new expiry.

Edit Templates Globally or Per Item

Want to edit your template globally for all future uses? Or just change it for a specific item?

Now you can do both:

Global Edit: Use the Templates section in GetSign settings.

Screenshot 2025-08-07 110450643×347 28.5 KB

Item-Level Edit: Use the edit icon inside the item to modify the template just for that case.

Screenshot 2025-08-07 110509641×327 25.4 KB

Search in the Template Gallery

Managing a growing number of templates? Use the new search bar in the template section to quickly find what you need. Just type in a keyword and matching templates will appear instantly.

Allow Board Members to Access Templates

By default, only board owners could access the template gallery. Now, with a simple toggle, you can let board members access the template gallery directly from the item view.

This makes it easy for teams to independently access the right document templates when they need them.

Screenshot 2025-08-07 1115081022×173 9.89 KB

These features are now live. Open GetSign on your monday board and try them out.

Have feedback or need help? Email us at appsupport@jetpackwork.com.

Keep using GetSign — the only document app you need for monday

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve been doing research into incorporating GetSign for our company, and overall, I think it’s one of the stronger apps available on Monday for sending documents out for signatures. That said, I’ve run into a few challenges that I wanted to share:

  1. Sub-item functionality – The ability to pull in sub-items is great, but it really only works smoothly when using Word-style (.docx) documents. My workflow relies heavily on PDFs, and when pulling sub-items into a PDF, the formatting/editing options are very limited, and the end result doesn’t look good.

  2. Template handling – Besides the template library, when I add a template to a deal, it automatically gets added to all deals. In my opinion, that shouldn’t be the case. If there’s a template library, that’s where templates should live, and then there should be an option to add a template to all deals or just one specific deal. I’m aware of the workaround (adding files from a column), but that requires extra manual steps like going into the file and re-placing signature fields.

  3. PDF editing limitations – The built-in PDF editor is very weak. For example, you can’t even add pages, which is something I find essential. Since my company primarily works with PDFs and product cut sheets, this is a big limitation. Compared to something like PandaDoc, which is very robust in this area, GetSign still has a long way to go.

Overall, I see great potential in GetSign, but these pain points (especially around PDFs) make it tough for teams like mine to rely on it fully.
